construct: Creating new paradigms vs. Optimizing within existing frameworks
positive_pole: Redefining fundamental assumptions about how markets, products, or interactions should work to create breakthrough possibilities
negative_pole: Accepting current market structures and competing through incremental improvements or efficiency gains
behavioral_implication: When facing industry conventions or user expectations, this person would challenge the underlying assumptions and create entirely new approaches rather than optimize within existing parameters

construct: Strategic ecosystem orchestration vs. Tactical competitive positioning
positive_pole: Managing multiple relationships and market dynamics simultaneously to create long-term strategic advantage
negative_pole: Focusing on immediate competitive wins or short-term tactical advantages without considering broader system effects
behavioral_implication: In competitive situations, this person would prioritize moves that strengthen the overall ecosystem and long-term positioning even if it means sacrificing immediate tactical advantages

construct: Controlled experience curation vs. Open platform dynamics
positive_pole: Maintaining tight control over user experience quality through selective partnerships and gatekeeping mechanisms
negative_pole: Allowing maximum openness and third-party access even at the cost of experience consistency or quality control
behavioral_implication: When designing platforms or partnerships, this person would choose mechanisms that preserve end-to-end quality control over those that maximize developer access or platform openness

positive_pole: Eliminating backward-looking elements that might constrain radical innovation and transformation
negative_pole: Preserving organizational history and legacy assets as sources of inspiration or competitive advantage
behavioral_implication: When organizational identity conflicts with transformation goals, this person would eliminate historical constraints rather than attempting to integrate past and future elements

construct: Brand authenticity through directness vs. Diplomatic relationship management
positive_pole: Expressing genuine views and positions even when they create tension, seeing authenticity as competitive advantage
negative_pole: Managing communications to maintain smooth relationships and avoid confrontation or controversy
behavioral_implication: In public communications about competitors or controversial topics, this person would prioritize authentic expression of their beliefs over diplomatic language that might preserve relationships

construct: Market creation through vision vs. Market validation through research
positive_pole: Using intuitive understanding of user needs and technological possibilities to create products that define new categories
negative_pole: Relying on systematic market research and customer feedback to guide product development decisions
behavioral_implication: When developing breakthrough products, this person would trust their design intuition and vision over market research data or customer requests

construct: Strategic expectation management vs. Transparent progress communication
positive_pole: Deliberately managing public expectations to preserve strategic flexibility and maximize surprise value
negative_pole: Providing accurate, real-time updates about progress and investments to maintain transparency with stakeholders
behavioral_implication: When launching experimental or high-stakes products, this person would use deliberately modest public positioning to create strategic maneuvering room rather than transparent progress updates

positive_pole: Eliminating complexity and cognitive burden even at the cost of advanced functionality or customization options
negative_pole: Providing maximum functionality and user control through comprehensive feature sets and configuration options
behavioral_implication: When faced with feature complexity, this person would consistently choose simpler user experiences over comprehensive functionality, even if it means limiting power user capabilities

construct: Commercial opportunity recognition vs. Technical achievement focus
positive_pole: Seeing market potential and scalable business models in technical innovations, naturally taking on sales and commercialization roles
negative_pole: Focusing primarily on technical excellence and peer validation without systematic attention to commercial viability
behavioral_implication: When encountering technical innovations, this person would immediately evaluate commercial potential and take initiative on business development rather than focusing solely on technical refinement

construct: Revolutionary capability building vs. Incremental resource optimization
positive_pole: Investing heavily in capabilities that enable breakthrough performance even when uncertain about immediate returns
negative_pole: Optimizing current resources and capabilities for maximum efficiency within existing operational parameters
behavioral_implication: When making investment decisions, this person would prioritize building revolutionary capabilities over optimizing current operations, even at significant financial risk
